title: 10 Soothing Herbal Teas to Help Combat Eczema
intro_desc: Herbal teas for eczema are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the symptoms of this chronic skin condition by soothing inflammation, reducing itching and promoting healing. The best herbal teas for eczema include Urtica dioica, Calendula officinalis, Aloe vera, Hypericum perforatum, Echinacea purpurea, Chamomilla recutita, Aloe barbadensis, Symphytum officinale, Camellia sinensis, and Melissa officinalis. If left untreated, eczema can significantly impact daily life, causing discomfort, anxiety, and sleep disturbances, ultimately affecting a person's overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tea is good for eczema because it has special properties that help soothe and calm the skin. The main active ingredient in calendula, called triterpenoids, has strong anti-inflammatory effects. This means it can reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema. Calendula also has antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infections that can make eczema worse. When you drink calendula tea, the antioxidants in it help to protect your skin from damage and promote healing. Calendula tea can also help to reduce itching and discomfort caused by eczema. Many people who suffer from eczema find relief by drinking calendula tea regularly, often in combination with topical creams or ointments. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to treat skin problems, including eczema, and it is considered a safe and effective way to manage symptoms.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for eczema because it helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin by acting as an antioxidant that prevents damage to skin cells and promotes healing.
Kaempferol is good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itching associated with eczema.
Tannins, also known as Comfrey, are good for eczema because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling, itching, and redness associated with eczema.

Saponins, also known as comfrey, are good for eczema because they have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce redness and itching of the skin, and promote healing of wounds and skin lesions.

Naringenin is good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe itchy skin, making it an effective natural remedy for calming and healing eczema symptoms.

remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal teas for eczema
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Symphytum officinale herbal tea, also known as comfrey tea, which can help with eczema, start by gathering the leaves and roots of the plant. You can either grow your own comfrey plant or purchase dried comfrey leaves and roots from a health food store. Once you have your comfrey, rinse the leaves and roots with fresh water to remove any dirt or debris. Then, place one teaspoon of the dried comfrey leaves and roots in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag. Pour boiling water over the comfrey and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. After the steeping time, remove the comfrey from the water and discard the solids. Let the tea cool down before drinking it. It is recommended to drink 1-2 cups of comfrey tea per day to help alleviate eczema symptoms. You can add honey or lemon to the tea if you prefer a sweeter or more flavorful taste. However, consult with a healthcare professional before using comfrey tea as a treatment for eczema, especially if you are pregnant or breastfeeding.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tea, also known as nettle tea, is a popular natural remedy for eczema. This is because the plant contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema. The tea also contains antioxidants, which protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promote healing. Additionally, nettle tea has been shown to soothe and calm itchy skin, providing relief from the discomfort and pain of eczema. The tea is also rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin C and iron, which are essential for maintaining healthy skin. Drinking nettle tea may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms. Many people have found that drinking nettle tea as part of their eczema treatment plan has helped to improve their symptoms and reduce their reliance on conventional medications.

remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal teas for eczema
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Symphytum officinale herbal tea that helps with eczema, start by gathering a few fresh or dried leaves of the plant, also known as comfrey. If using fresh leaves, gently wash them with cold water to remove any dirt. You can use a tea infuser or a small muslin bag to hold the leaves while brewing. If you don't have a tea infuser, you can simply place the leaves in a cup. Next, boil some water and let it cool down a bit, then pour it over the comfrey leaves. Allow the mixture to steep for about 5-7 minutes, depending on your desired strength of tea. Once the leaves have infused, strain the liquid and discard the leaves. You can now drink the comfrey tea as is, or add a bit of honey to sweeten it. It's recommended to drink 1-2 cups of comfrey tea per day to help alleviate eczema symptoms, but always consult with a healthcare professional before making any changes to your diet.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tea has been used for centuries to help soothe and calm eczema. Eczema is a skin condition that causes red, itchy, and painful patches. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and redness of the skin. The gel from the aloe vera plant contains a group of compounds called anthraquinones, which have been shown to have anti-itch and anti-inflammatory effects. When you drink aloe vera tea, these compounds are absorbed into your body, where they can help to calm and soothe the skin from the inside out. This can be especially helpful for people who have eczema on their hands or feet, as it can be difficult to apply creams or ointments to these areas. Drinking aloe vera tea may also help to reduce the frequency and severity of eczema outbreaks. Additionally, aloe vera tea is rich in vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin A and vitamin C, which are important for healthy skin.

remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal teas for eczema
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Symphytum officinale herbal tea that helps with eczema, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You can buy dried Symphytum officinale leaves from a health food store or online. If you have access to a garden, you can also grow and harvest the plant yourself, making sure to only pick the leaves from a healthy plant. Next, combine 1-2 teaspoons of dried Symphytum officinale leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or mug. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on your desired strength of tea. After steeping, strain the tea leaves from the liquid and discard them. You can drink the tea hot or cold, sweetened or unsweetened, and it's recommended to drink it 2-3 times a day for optimal benefits. It's also essential to note that before using Symphytum officinale tea for eczema, you should consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist to ensure it won't interact with any medications or worsen your condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea is good for eczema because it has soothing and healing properties. The leaves of the aloe plant contain a gel-like substance that is rich in vitamins and minerals, particularly vitamin E and beta-carotene. These nutrients help to calm and moisturize the skin, reducing inflammation and redness that are common symptoms of eczema. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe barbadensis also help to reduce itching and irritation, giving people with eczema relief from the discomfort and pain. When consumed as a tea, aloe barbadensis can help to promote skin health from the inside out, reducing the severity of eczema symptoms. It also supports the body's natural healing processes, helping to repair damaged skin and promote new skin cell growth.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Symphytum officinale herbal tea that helps with eczema, you need a few simple ingredients and steps. First, buy dried Symphytum officinale leaves or roots from a health food store, as they are the active ingredients that help to soothe eczema. Next, place one teaspoon of the dried leaves or roots in a cup and pour in 8 ounces of boiling water. Let it steep for 5-10 minutes, allowing the active ingredients to dissolve into the water. Once the tea has cooled, you can strain it using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids. Finally, drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally after bathing or showering to help lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy skin. It's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using Symphytum officinale tea for eczema treatment, especially if you have sensitive skin or allergies.

Some people find that drinking the tea helps to calm the skin and reduce itching and irritation. Additionally, the tea has been used for centuries to treat skin conditions, and some studies suggest that it may be effective in reducing the severity of eczema symptoms.

These compounds help reduce swelling and redness on the skin, making it a soothing treatment for itchy and irritated skin. The tea also has antioxidant properties, which protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. This helps to calm and quiet the skin, reducing the urge to scratch and further irritate the skin. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Symphytum officinale herbal tea that helps with eczema, start by gathering a handful of fresh or dried comfrey leaves, roots, and flowers. If using fresh, clean and chop them into small pieces. In a medium saucepan, combine the chopped comfrey and one liter of water, making sure the herbs are completely covered. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to a gentle simmer for 5-10 minutes. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it steep for 5-10 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their healing properties. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl, discarding the solids. Your comfrey tea is now ready to use. You can drink it warm or chilled, sweetened with honey if desired. For eczema relief, it's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of comfrey tea per day.

These compounds may help reduce redness and swelling often associated with eczema. The tea also contains antioxidants that may help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Drinking dandelion tea may also help to balance the body's pH levels and reduce inflammation, which can be beneficial for people with eczema. Additionally, dandelion tea is rich in vitamins and minerals like vitamin C and potassium, which are essential for healthy skin. Some people find that drinking dandelion tea regularly helps to calm and soothe their skin, reducing the severity of eczema symptoms. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness in treating eczema.

supplementary_best: Combining 'Calendula officinalis', 'Aloe vera', and 'Symphytum officinale' in a herbal tea may help remedy eczema. 'Calendula officinalis' has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce skin swelling and redness associated with eczema. 'Aloe vera' is known for its soothing and moisturizing effects, which can calm the skin and reduce itching. 'Symphytum officinale', or comfrey, contains allantoin, a compound that promotes skin cell growth and reduces skin irritation. Together, these herbs can provide a powerful combination to alleviate eczema symptoms. Drinking this tea may help reduce inflammation, soothe the skin, and promote healthy skin cell growth, making it a potentially effective remedy for eczema.
supplementary_avoid: If you have eczema, you should avoid drinking herbal teas made from certain herbs that can irritate or trigger eczema symptoms. One such herb is goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis), which may cause skin irritation and make eczema worse. Another herb to avoid is goldthread (Coptis chinensis), which is also in the goldenseal family and has similar properties.  Teas made from ginkgo biloba can also be problematic. The sap of the ginkgo biloba tree can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some people.  Teas made from sage (Salvia officinalis) may also not be suitable for people with eczema. Sage can cause skin irritation and make eczema symptoms worse.  You should also be cautious with teas made from licorice root (Glycyrrhiza glabra). While licorice root is sometimes used to treat skin conditions, it can cause skin irritation and worsen eczema symptoms in some people.  Additionally, teas made from saw palmetto (Serenoa repens) may not be suitable for people with eczema. Saw palmetto can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some people.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel can help soothe and calm eczema skin. It has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling, making it easier for skin to heal. Aloe vera is also rich in vitamins and minerals that help lock in moisture, keeping the skin hydrated and soft.  Oatmeal baths are another natural remedy for eczema. The starch in oats helps to absorb excess moisture from the skin, reducing itchiness and discomfort. Oatmeal also has anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the skin.  Topical coconut oil can be applied directly to eczema skin to provide relief. Coconut oil is rich in fatty acids that help to lock in moisture and soothe dry skin. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling.  Apple cider vinegar can be added to bath water to help alleviate eczema symptoms. The acidity in apple cider vinegar helps to balance the skin's pH, which can become imbalanced due to eczema. This can help to reduce itchiness and discomfort.  Probiotics can be taken orally to help alleviate eczema symptoms. Probiotics help to maintain a healthy balance of gut bacteria, which is linked to a healthy immune system and healthy skin. A healthy gut and immune system can help to reduce inflammation and itchiness associated with eczema.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with eczema often experience other skin issues and health problems. One common condition is dry skin, which can be helped by using herbal remedies like aloe vera and chamomile. These plants have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the skin, reducing itching and redness.  Another condition associated with eczema is asthma, which can be caused by inflammation in the airways. Herbal remedies like butterbur and marshmallow root have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.  Some people with eczema may also experience allergies, which can be triggered by certain foods or environmental factors. Herbal remedies like quercetin and stinging nettle can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of allergies like itchy eyes and runny nose.  In addition to these conditions, people with eczema may also experience digestive problems like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal remedies like peppermint and ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe digestive issues and reduce symptoms of IBS like bloating and abdominal pain.  Lastly, some people with eczema may experience anxiety and depression, which can be triggered by the emotional and physical discomfort of living with eczema. Herbal remedies like passionflower and lavender have calming properties that can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, leading to improved mental health.
